name: Severe bleeding
summary: Heavy blood loss from a wound. Direct pressure stops blood flow; without it, shock and death follow.
recognition: Large gash from a sharp object

steps: Grab any non-fibrous cloth (T-shirt is fine, bigger the better).
Apply direct pressure to the wound to stop blood flow.
A compression bandage (wrapped very tightly) does the same job.
Raise the wound above the heart if possible — reduces pressure and bleeding.
Keep pressure on until help arrives.
Monitor for shock (greyish skin, fast shallow breathing, sweating).
Do NOT remove the object — it may be stemming the bleeding.
Don't put pressure ON the object.
Use sterile dressing and bandages to BUILD UP around the object.
This applies pressure around the wound and supports the object.

warnings.text: Don't remove an embedded object — what cuts going in will cut going out.
A slash wound bleeds dramatically. An impale wound may bleed INTERNALLY and is more dangerous — call 999 even if it doesn't look bad outside.
